curation_notes: Re-curated with updated L3 hints. Locations derived from the patch diff. The primary vulnerability is in src/sequelize.js in the query method, which called Utils.format and Utils.formatNamedParameters to perform naive string-based replacement of positional (?) and named (:name) parameters. These utility functions in src/utils.js did not parse SQL structure (strings, comments, column identifiers), so user values containing replacement markers could trigger a second substitution pass. The patch replaced these calls with a new injectReplacements function that properly parses SQL token boundaries before substituting. Six non-test files were changed (including type definitions, package.json, and a new utility file), making this a noisy patch. No sibling files were found using the same vulnerable pattern. Vulnerability class sql-injection confirmed: CWE-89 matches, advisory explicitly states SQL injection, and the patch fixes unsanitized SQL replacement processing.
